enonce.texte: Match the vocabulary words related to African innovation with their correct definitions. These terms are essential for understanding texts about African inventors and their creations.
images: A collage of African inventions including a smartphone, a solar panel, and a medical device

questions.texte: Match each term with its correct definition:

sousQuestions.texte: Inventor
sousQuestions.reponse: b) A person who creates or designs something new

sousQuestions.texte: Device
sousQuestions.reponse: c) A piece of equipment or machinery designed for a specific purpose

sousQuestions.texte: Technology
sousQuestions.reponse: a) The application of scientific knowledge for practical purposes

sousQuestions.texte: Solution
sousQuestions.reponse: e) A way of solving a problem or dealing with a difficult situation

sousQuestions.texte: Development
sousQuestions.reponse: d) The process of growing, changing, or improving something

intitule: Simple Past Tense in Innovation Contexts

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences using the simple past tense of the verbs in brackets. The simple past is used to talk about past achievements and completed actions, which is important when describing inventions and innovations.
images: A timeline showing key African inventions from the 2000s to the present day

questions.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct simple past form:

sousQuestions.texte: Arthur Zang _____ (invent) the Cardio Pad in Cameroon.
sousQuestions.reponse: invented

sousQuestions.texte: The company _____ (develop) a new mobile payment system.
sousQuestions.reponse: developed

sousQuestions.texte: She _____ (create) a solar-powered water pump for rural areas.
sousQuestions.reponse: created

sousQuestions.texte: The engineers _____ (design) a low-cost ventilation system.
sousQuestions.reponse: designed

sousQuestions.texte: They _____ (launch) their product at the technology conference.
sousQuestions.reponse: launched

intitule: Reading: Arthur Zang and the Cardio Pad

enonce.texte:
Read the text about Arthur Zang, a Cameroonian inventor, and his invention, the Cardio Pad. Then answer the questions that follow.

Arthur Zang: A Cameroonian Innovator

Arthur Zang is a young Cameroonian engineer who invented the Cardio Pad, a revolutionary medical device. He was born in 1987 in Cameroon. He studied at the Polytechnic University of Yaoundé, where he obtained a degree in computer engineering. After his studies, he worked as an IT engineer. However, he had a vision: to improve healthcare in Africa.

In 2010, he invented the Cardio Pad, a tablet that allows doctors to perform heart examinations in remote areas. The device was designed to be affordable and easy to use. It can connect to a smartphone and send medical data to specialists in city hospitals. This invention helps to reduce the time and cost of medical examinations for patients who live far from specialized clinics.

Arthur Zang received several awards for his invention. He was recognized as one of the most innovative young Africans. His story inspires many young people to pursue careers in science and technology.
images: Arthur Zang, a young Cameroonian engineer, holding the Cardio Pad tablet in a rural health center

questions.texte: Read the text and answer the following questions about Arthur Zang and his invention.
questions.reponse: Answers will vary, but should demonstrate understanding of the text.

sousQuestions.texte: What is Arthur Zang known for?
sousQuestions.reponse: He is known for inventing the Cardio Pad.

sousQuestions.texte: When did Arthur Zang invent the Cardio Pad?
Identify the purpose of the Cardio Pad.
sousQuestions.reponse: The Cardio Pad is a tablet that allows doctors to perform heart examinations in remote areas.

sousQuestions.texte: Analyze the impact of the Cardio Pad on healthcare in Africa.
sousQuestions.reponse: The Cardio Pad helps to reduce the time and cost of medical examinations for patients who live far from specialized clinics. It also helps doctors to connect with specialists in city hospitals.

enonce.texte: Listen to the audio recording about African innovations (the teacher will play it). The recording features stories of young African inventors. After listening, answer the following comprehension questions.
images: A group of young African entrepreneurs discussing their innovative projects around a table in a modern workspace

questions.texte: Recall and organize the key information from the recording.
questions.reponse: The recording discusses how young Africans are using technology to solve local problems. It mentions inventors who have created low-cost solutions in healthcare, agriculture, and energy.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the main theme of the recording?
sousQuestions.reponse: African innovations and young inventors.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify one invention mentioned in the recording.
sousQuestions.reponse: The recording mentions a solar-powered water pump and a mobile payment system.

sousQuestions.texte: Analyze the common challenges these inventors face.
sousQuestions.reponse: They often face challenges such as lack of funding, limited access to technology, and difficulty in bringing their products to market.

intitule: Writing: Profile of an African Innovator

enonce.texte:
Write a short essay (150-200 words) profiling an inspiring contemporary African personality. You may choose from the following list or select another African innovator you know:

  • Arthur Zang (Cameroon - Cardio Pad)
  • Nneile Nkosi (South Africa - 3D-printed prosthetic limbs)
  • Agnes Binagwaho (Rwanda - Healthcare innovator)
  • Erik Hersman (Kenya - BRCK Education)
images: A portrait gallery of African innovators, including Arthur Zang, Nneile Nkosi, and others

questions.texte: Write a profile of an African innovator. Include information about their background, their achievements, and the impact of their work.
questions.reponse: Arthur Zang is a Cameroonian engineer who invented the Cardio Pad, a medical tablet for heart examinations. Born in 1987, he studied at the Polytechnic University of Yaoundé. He wanted to improve healthcare in remote areas where patients could not access specialized clinics. His invention has helped thousands of people receive heart examinations quickly and at low cost. Arthur Zang's work has inspired many young Africans to pursue careers in technology. His story shows that innovation can come from anywhere, and that young people can make a big difference in the world.

enonce.texte: Match the African innovators with their inventions. This exercise tests your knowledge of some of the most innovative Africans in recent years.
images: A world map highlighting Africa with icons representing different inventions like solar panels, medical devices, and mobile payment systems

questions.texte: Match the innovator with their invention:

sousQuestions.texte: Arthur Zang
sousQuestions.reponse: b) Cardio Pad

sousQuestions.texte: William Kamkwamba
sousQuestions.reponse: d) Windmill made from scrap materials

sousQuestions.texte: Nneile Nkosi
sousQuestions.reponse: a) 3D-printed prosthetics

sousQuestions.texte: Mo Ibrahim
sousQuestions.reponse: c) Mobile telecommunication company

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences using the simple past tense. Pay attention to the correct form of the verbs and the context of each sentence. This exercise will help you describe innovations and achievements accurately.
images: A young woman working on a computer with images of gears and innovative ideas around her, suggesting innovation

questions.texte: Rewrite these sentences in the simple past tense:
questions.reponse: 1. He invented a device that helps heart examinations. 2. They developed a mobile phone app. 3. She designed a solar-powered water pump. 4. We created a new product for our community.

sousQuestions.texte: He invents a device that helps heart examinations.
sousQuestions.reponse: He invented a device that helps heart examinations.

sousQuestions.texte: They develop a mobile phone app.
sousQuestions.reponse: They developed a mobile phone app.

sousQuestions.texte: She designs a solar-powered water pump.
sousQuestions.reponse: She designed a solar-powered water pump.

sousQuestions.texte: We create a new product for our community.
sousQuestions.reponse: We created a new product for our community.
